analysis:
The CLI app lives at apps/cli/src/. The core config module (config.ts) already implements both findProjectConfig and resolveContext — confirmed by reading the source and by git history (US-001 and US-002 are complete as of the last three commits on feat/project-cli-config). Both functions are fully tested in config.spec.ts.

Key existing patterns:
- All commands use `resolveAuth({})` from utils/auth.ts for apiKey/apiUrl — this does NOT handle projectSlug or project-level config.
- Project slug in every command is resolved as: `options.project || process.env['GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG'] || 'koda'` — hardcoded fallback, no auto-detection.
- commands/config.ts exposes configShow/configSet; it has no `profile` sub-commands.
- index.ts wires the `config` command inline using Commander addCommand — profile sub-commands must be added here or via a refactored configCommand helper.
- The generated HTTP client is used via TicketsService, ProjectsService etc. from src/generated (not manually written). The init command will need ProjectsService to validate the slug against the API.
- Exit codes are strict: 0 success, 1 API error, 2 config/auth error, 3 validation error.

Risks:
- ticket.ts is 22KB with 15+ sub-commands; every action block must become async if not already (they already are). Replacing resolveAuth with resolveContext changes auth+url resolution to also handle projectSlug — ensure apiKey check still gates all API calls.
- Removing GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG must be a complete removal (the env var is a fallback in every command file: ticket.ts, comment.ts, label.ts, kb.ts, agent.ts).
- koda init writes .koda/config.json in cwd — the directory must be created (mkdir -p .koda) before writing.
- Profile management in config.ts must use setConfig with a profiles patch; the Conf store already supports a profiles object.
- index.ts currently registers `config` inline; adding `profile` sub-commands requires either extending the inline block or moving to a configCommand() function pattern matching other commands.

Dependency order: US-001 → US-002 → US-003 → US-004/US-005 (US-004 and US-005 are independent of each other).

userStories.title: findProjectConfig walk-up utility
userStories.description: Implement findProjectConfig(dir?) in src/config.ts. Walks up from dir (defaults to process.cwd()) looking for .koda/config.json. Returns parsed ProjectConfig or null if not found or JSON is invalid.
acceptanceCriteria: findProjectConfig('/a/b/c') returns ProjectConfig when .koda/config.json exists at /a/b/c/.koda/config.json
findProjectConfig('/a/b/c') returns ProjectConfig when .koda/config.json exists at /a/.koda/config.json (walk-up finds ancestor)
findProjectConfig('/a/b/c') returns null when no .koda/config.json exists anywhere up to filesystem root
findProjectConfig() defaults dir to process.cwd() when called with no argument
findProjectConfig returns null and does not throw when .koda/config.json exists but contains invalid JSON

routing.reasoning: Single function, <50 LOC, additive change to config.ts. Already implemented and verified.

userStories.title: resolveContext merge logic
userStories.description: Implement resolveContext(flags, deps?) in src/config.ts. Merges CLI flags, project config (.koda/config.json), active profile from global config, and global config according to resolution priority: flags > .koda/ > profile > global > defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveContext({ projectSlug: 'override' }) returns projectSlug: 'override' even when .koda/config.json has a different projectSlug
resolveContext({}) returns projectSlug from .koda/config.json when no flag is passed and project config exists
resolveContext({}) returns apiUrl and apiKey from the named profile when .koda/config.json has 'profile': 'staging' and that profile exists in global config
resolveContext({}) returns apiUrl from .koda/config.json when it overrides the global apiUrl and no CLI flag is passed
resolveContext({}) returns apiKey from global config when .koda/config.json has no profile field
resolveContext({}) returns apiUrl: 'http://localhost:3100/api' and apiKey: '' when no config files exist

routing.reasoning: Multi-level merge logic across 3 config sources with 6 acceptance criteria. Already implemented and verified.

userStories.title: Wire resolveContext into all commands
userStories.description: Replace the `options.project || process.env['GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG'] || 'koda'` pattern and `resolveAuth({})` calls in all commands (ticket.ts, comment.ts, label.ts, kb.ts, agent.ts) with resolveContext(options). Remove the GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG env var fallback entirely. When projectSlug is undefined after resolution, exit with code 2 and print 'Project not configured. Run: koda init'.
acceptanceCriteria: ticketCommand('list') action calls TicketsService with the projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted and .koda/config.json has projectSlug: 'koda'
ticketCommand('list') action calls TicketsService with projectSlug: 'foo' when --project foo is passed, regardless of what .koda/config.json contains
ticketCommand('list') action exits with code 2 and logs 'Project not configured. Run: koda init' when no .koda/config.json exists and --project flag is omitted
commentCommand action resolves projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted
labelCommand action resolves projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted
agentCommand action resolves projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted
No reference to GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG exists in any command file after this change

routing.reasoning: Touches 5 command files including ticket.ts (22KB, 15+ sub-commands). All command actions must be updated; async behavior must be preserved. Missing projectSlug must produce correct exit code/message. High risk of regression — strict TDD isolation required.

userStories.title: Wire resolveContext into ticketCommand with undefined guard
userStories.description: Replace the `options.project || process.env['GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG'] || 'koda'` pattern and `resolveAuth({})` call in ticket.ts with `resolveContext(options)`. After resolution, if projectSlug is undefined, exit with code 2 and print 'Project not configured. Run: koda init'. This establishes the canonical pattern for all other command migrations.
acceptanceCriteria: ticketCommand('list') action calls TicketsService with the projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted and .koda/config.json has projectSlug: 'koda'
ticketCommand('list') action calls TicketsService with projectSlug: 'foo' when --project foo is passed, regardless of what .koda/config.json contains
ticketCommand('list') action exits with code 2 and logs 'Project not configured. Run: koda init' when no .koda/config.json exists and --project flag is omitted
ticket.ts contains no reference to GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG after this change

routing.reasoning: Refactoring one file with pattern replacement + new guard logic + 4 specific test cases to verify; straightforward but requires implementation verification.

userStories.description: Replace the project/auth resolution pattern in comment.ts with `resolveContext(options)`. Apply the same undefined-projectSlug guard established in US-003-1 (exit code 2, print error message).
acceptanceCriteria: commentCommand action resolves projectSlug from .koda/config.json when --project flag is omitted
commentCommand action exits with code 2 and logs 'Project not configured. Run: koda init' when no .koda/config.json exists and --project flag is omitted
comment.ts contains no reference to GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG after this change

routing.reasoning: Straightforward refactor applying established US-003-1 pattern to commentCommand; copy-paste across one file with no new behavior.

userStories.title: Remove GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG from kb.ts and perform global audit
userStories.description: Apply resolveContext to kb.ts (if it follows the same pattern) and perform a global grep audit across all command files to confirm zero remaining references to GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG. Fail the story if any reference survives after US-003-1 through US-003-4.
acceptanceCriteria: kb.ts uses resolveContext(options) for project and auth resolution
No reference to GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG exists in any file under apps/cli/src/commands/ after this change
A grep for 'GLOBAL_PROJECT_SLUG' across the CLI source tree returns zero matches

userStories.title: koda init command
userStories.description: Implement `koda init` in src/commands/init.ts and register it in index.ts. Non-interactive: `koda init --project <slug>` fetches the project from the API to validate it exists, then writes .koda/config.json in cwd. Supports --default-type and --default-priority options. Idempotent (overwrites on second run). Prints a reminder that the file must not contain tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: initCommand({ project: 'koda' }) writes { "projectSlug": "koda" } to .koda/config.json in the current working directory without prompting
initCommand({ project: 'koda' }) exits with code 0 and prints a line containing '✓ Created .koda/config.json'
initCommand({ project: 'koda' }) called a second time in the same directory overwrites .koda/config.json with updated values
initCommand({ project: 'nonexistent' }) exits with code 1 and prints 'Project not found: nonexistent' when the API returns 404
initCommand({ project: 'koda', defaultType: 'BUG', defaultPriority: 'HIGH' }) writes { "projectSlug": "koda", "defaults": { "type": "BUG", "priority": "HIGH" } } to .koda/config.json
initCommand({}) when no auth is configured exits with code 2 and prints 'Not logged in. Run: koda login --api-key <key>'

routing.reasoning: New command file (~100 LOC), API validation call, filesystem write with mkdir -p, 6 ACs covering success/error/idempotent paths. Clear requirements and standard command pattern.

userStories.title: koda config profile sub-commands
userStories.description: Implement `koda config profile list`, `koda config profile add`, and `koda config profile remove` sub-commands. Add getProfiles, setProfile, removeProfile helpers to src/config.ts. Register the profile sub-command under the existing `config` command in index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: setProfile('staging', { apiUrl: 'https://staging.koda.io/api', apiKey: 'stg-xxx' }) writes { apiUrl, apiKey } under profiles.staging in the global config store
configProfileList() returns an array of { name, apiUrl } entries for each stored profile
configProfileList() returns an empty array when profiles is empty or absent in global config
removeProfile('staging') removes profiles.staging from the global config store and returns without error
removeProfile('nonexistent') throws an error with message containing 'Profile not found: nonexistent'
setProfile('staging', { apiUrl: '...', apiKey: 'new-key' }) called twice with different apiKey values stores only the second value under profiles.staging
The `koda config profile list` CLI action prints a row for each profile with Name and ApiUrl columns and exits with code 0
The `koda config profile list` CLI action prints 'No profiles configured' and exits with code 0 when no profiles exist
The `koda config profile remove nonexistent` CLI action exits with code 1 and prints 'Profile not found: nonexistent'

routing.reasoning: 3 new sub-commands plus 3 helper functions in config.ts (~120 LOC total across 2-3 files). Standard CRUD pattern on the profiles object in the Conf store. No new dependencies required.
